methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Scout AI is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Colby Adcock and Collin Otis that has raised $15M to date. The broader defense and security sector contains 19 comparable companies across public and private markets, including 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI. However, the scarcity of seed-stage or similarly-capitalized peers in this specialized sector limits direct comparability.

Given data constraints, valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $15M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $38M. This single-method approach produces a triangulated range of $32M (low) to $43M (high), with a median estimate of $38M.

The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single valuation method rather than multiple independent approaches. A peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient comparable companies at the same stage and valuation tier within the defense and security sector. Validation of this range would require either identification of additional seed-stage defense tech comparables or forward-looking metrics on customer traction and deployment momentum that were not available in the current dataset.
